sourceQuestion:
A superadditive function f(-) satisfies the following property

f(x_1 + x_2) >= f(x_1) + f(x_2)

Which of the following functions is a superadditive function for x >= 1?

A. e^x
B. sqrt(x)
C. 1/x
D. e^(-x)

parsed.stem:
A superadditive function f(-) satisfies the following property

f(x_1 + x_2) >= f(x_1) + f(x_2)

Which of the following functions is a superadditive function for x >= 1?

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following options represents the given graph?

[Graph shows a smooth bell-shaped curve with a single peak above the x-axis, symmetric about x = 0, approaching zero as x -> +/-inf]

A. f(x) = x * 2^(-x^2)
B. f(x) = x * 2^(|x|)
C. f(x) = |x| * 2^(-x^2)
D. f(x) = x * 2^(-x)

parsed.stem:
Which one of the following options represents the given graph?

[Graph shows a smooth bell-shaped curve with a single peak above the x-axis, symmetric about x = 0, approaching zero as x -> +/-inf]

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following options represents the given graph?

A. f(x) = x * 2^(-x)
B. f(x) = x * 2^(-|x|)
C. f(x) = |x| * 2^(-x)
D. f(x) = x * 2^(-x) (for x >= 0 only)

parsed.stem: Which one of the following options represents the given graph?

sourceQuestion:
Ankita has to climb 5 stairs starting at the ground, while respecting the following rules:
1. At any stage, Ankita can move either one or two stairs up.
2. At any stage, Ankita cannot move to a lower step.

Let F(N) denote the number of possible ways in which Ankita can reach the N^th stair. For example, F(1) = 1, F(2) = 2, F(3) = 3.

The value of F(5) is:

A. 5
B. 7
C. 6
D. 5

parsed.stem:
Ankita has to climb 5 stairs starting at the ground, while respecting the following rules:
1. At any stage, Ankita can move either one or two stairs up.
2. At any stage, Ankita cannot move to a lower step.

Let F(N) denote the number of possible ways in which Ankita can reach the N^th stair. For example, F(1) = 1, F(2) = 2, F(3) = 3.

The value of F(5) is:

sourceQuestion:
Define [z] as the greatest integer less than or equal to z, for each z in (-inf, +inf). If y = [x], then area under g for x in [1, 3] is

Where f(x) = x - [x] and g(x) = f(x) for x in [1, 3].

A. 1
B. 3
C. 6
D. 6

parsed.stem:
Define [z] as the greatest integer less than or equal to z, for each z in (-inf, +inf). If y = [x], then area under g for x in [1, 3] is

Where f(x) = x - [x] and g(x) = f(x) for x in [1, 3].

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following for non-zero positive integers p and q:

f(p, q) = f(p, q-1) * p    [recursive definition, with base f(p, 1) = p]
g(p, q) = g(p-1, q)        [with base g(1, q) = q]
And: g(p, 1) = p

Which one of the following options is correct based on the above?

A. f(2,2) < f(2,3)
B. f(2,2) < f(3,2)
C. f(2,3) < f(3,2)
D. f(3,2) < f(2,3)

parsed.stem:
Consider the following for non-zero positive integers p and q:

f(p, q) = f(p, q-1) * p    [recursive definition, with base f(p, 1) = p]
g(p, q) = g(p-1, q)        [with base g(1, q) = q]
And: g(p, 1) = p

Which one of the following options is correct based on the above?

sourceQuestion:
Given the following four functions, which function will have the largest value for sufficiently large values of n (1 < n -> infinity)?

A. f_1(n) = n^(sqrt(n))
B. f_2(n) = 2^n
C. f_3(n) = n^(log_2 n)
D. f_4(n) = n^10

parsed.stem: Given the following four functions, which function will have the largest value for sufficiently large values of n (1 < n -> infinity)?

sourceQuestion:
Let f(x) = z - |p|, where z >= 0 and |p| is the greatest integer not larger than z. Then f(x) is a:

A. monotonically increasing function
B. monotonically decreasing function
C. linearly increasing function between two integers
D. linearly decreasing function between two integers
sourceAnswer: C. linearly increasing function between two integers

parsed.stem: Let f(x) = z - |p|, where z >= 0 and |p| is the greatest integer not larger than z. Then f(x) is a:

options.text: linearly increasing function between two integers
linearly decreasing function between two integers

sourceQuestion:
If x > y > 1, which of the following must be true?

i. ln x > ln y
ii. e^x > e^y
iii. y^x > x^y
iv. cos x > cos y

A. (i) and (ii)
B. (i) and (iii)
C. (iii) and (iv)
D. (ii) and (iv)

parsed.stem:
If x > y > 1, which of the following must be true?

i. ln x > ln y
ii. e^x > e^y
iii. y^x > x^y
iv. cos x > cos y

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following curves represents the function y = ln(|e^(|x| - pi)|) for |x| <= 2*pi? Here, x represents the abscissa and y represents the ordinate.

A. (curve showing V-shape touching zero at x = +/-pi with smooth symmetric profile)
B. (curve with two peaks and a sharp dip)
C. (curve with |x|-pi shape: V-shaped touching zero at +/-pi, linear rise on both sides)
D. (curve with inverted shape)
sourceAnswer: C. (the curve showing absolute value shape: rising linearly away from x = +/-pi, touching y=0 at x=+/-pi)

parsed.stem: Which of the following curves represents the function y = ln(|e^(|x| - pi)|) for |x| <= 2*pi? Here, x represents the abscissa and y represents the ordinate.

options.text: (curve showing V-shape touching zero at x = +/-pi with smooth symmetric profile)
(curve with two peaks and a sharp dip)
(curve with |x|-pi shape: V-shaped touching zero at +/-pi, linear rise on both sides)

sourceQuestion:
F(a, b) and G(a, b) are defined as follows:
F(a, b) = (a - b)^2
G(a, b) = |a - b|, where |x| represents the absolute value of x.

What would be the value of G(F(1, 3), G(1, 3))?

A. 2   B. 4   C. 6   D. 36

parsed.stem:
F(a, b) and G(a, b) are defined as follows:
F(a, b) = (a - b)^2
G(a, b) = |a - b|, where |x| represents the absolute value of x.

What would be the value of G(F(1, 3), G(1, 3))?

A. P = 6, Q = 5, R = 3
B. P = 5, Q = 6, R = 3
C. P = 3, Q = 3, R = 6
D. P = 3, Q = 5, R = 6

sourceQuestion:

The number of squares that can be formed and the number of triangles required to form each square, respectively, are:

A. 3, 4
B. 4, 3
C. 3, 3
D. 3, 2

sourceQuestion:
A trapezium has vertices marked as P, Q, R and S (in that order anticlockwise). The side PQ is parallel to side SR. Further, it is given that PQ = 11 cm, QR = 4 cm, RS = 6 cm and SP = 3 cm. What is the shortest distance between PQ and SR (in cm)?

A. 1.80   B. 2.40   C. 4.20   D. 5.76

sourceQuestion:
Four identical cylindrical chalk-sticks, each of radius r = 0.5 cm and length l = 10 cm, are bound tightly together using a duct tape as shown in the following figure. The width of the duct tape is equal to the length of the chalk-stick. The area (in cm^2) of the duct tape required to wrap the bundle of chalk-sticks once is:

A. 2(4 + pi)   B. 20(4 + pi)   C. 10(8 + pi)   D. 10(4 + pi)

sourceQuestion:
A rhombus is formed by joining the midpoints of the sides of a unit square. What is the diameter of the largest circle that can be inscribed within the rhombus?

A. 1/sqrt(2)   B. 1/sqrt(2)   C. sqrt(2)   D. 2*sqrt(2)

parsed.stem: A rhombus is formed by joining the midpoints of the sides of a unit square. What is the diameter of the largest circle that can be inscribed within the rhombus?

sourceQuestion:
A square pyramid has a base perimeter b, and the slant height is half of the perimeter. What is the lateral surface area of the pyramid?

A. b^2/2  B. 0.75*b^2  C. 0.50*b^2  D. 0.25*b^2

parsed.stem: A square pyramid has a base perimeter b, and the slant height is half of the perimeter. What is the lateral surface area of the pyramid?

sourceQuestion:
A wire of length 36 mm is to be cut into two parts. One of the parts is to be made into a square and the other into a rectangle where sides are in the ratio of 1:2. What is the length of the side of the square (in mm) such that the combined area of the square and the rectangle is a MINIMUM?

A. 30  B. 40  C. 120  D. 180

parsed.stem: A wire of length 36 mm is to be cut into two parts. One of the parts is to be made into a square and the other into a rectangle where sides are in the ratio of 1:2. What is the length of the side of the square (in mm) such that the combined area of the square and the rectangle is a MINIMUM?

sourceQuestion:
A window is made up of a square portion and an equilateral triangle portion above it. The base of the triangular portion coincides with the upper side of the square. If the perimeter of the window is 6 m, the area of the window in m^2 is ___________.

A. 1.43  B. 2.06  C. 2.68  D. 2.88

parsed.stem: A window is made up of a square portion and an equilateral triangle portion above it. The base of the triangular portion coincides with the upper side of the square. If the perimeter of the window is 6 m, the area of the window in m^2 is ___________.

sourceQuestion:
A right-angled cone (with base radius 5 cm and height 12 cm), as shown in the figure below, is rolled on the ground keeping the point P fixed until the point Q (at the base of the cone, as shown) touches the ground again. By what angle (in radians) about P does the cone travel?

A. 5*pi/13  B. 10*pi/13  C. 6*pi/13  D. 5*pi/12

parsed.stem: A right-angled cone (with base radius 5 cm and height 12 cm), as shown in the figure below, is rolled on the ground keeping the point P fixed until the point Q (at the base of the cone, as shown) touches the ground again. By what angle (in radians) about P does the cone travel?

sourceQuestion:
Arrange the following three-dimensional objects in the descending order of their volumes:
 i. A cuboid with dimensions 10 cm, 8 cm and height 5 cm
 ii. A cube of side 9 cm
 iii. A cylinder with base radius 7 cm and height 7 cm
 iv. A sphere of radius 7 cm

A. (i), (ii), (iii), (iv)
B. (iv), (iii), (ii), (i)
C. (iii), (iv), (i), (ii)
D. (iv), (iii), (i), (ii)

parsed.stem:
Arrange the following three-dimensional objects in the descending order of their volumes:
 i. A cuboid with dimensions 10 cm, 8 cm and height 5 cm
 ii. A cube of side 9 cm
 iii. A cylinder with base radius 7 cm and height 7 cm
 iv. A sphere of radius 7 cm

sourceQuestion:
A set of 4 parallel lines intersect with another set of 5 parallel lines. How many parallelograms are formed?

A. 20
B. 48
C. 60
D. 72

parsed.stem: A set of 4 parallel lines intersect with another set of 5 parallel lines. How many parallelograms are formed?
